overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under CA Civil Code §1671
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor's LD claims on early termination may not hold up in CA courts

overrides.override_summary: CA Business & Professions Code §§20000-20043 provide rights concerning termination or non-renewal that supersede FA
overrides.why_it_matters: Additional protections for CA franchisees on termination/non-renewal beyond what FA provides

overrides.override_summary: FA provision requiring NY law may not be enforceable under CA law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisee may be able to apply CA law instead of NY law

overrides.override_summary: FA provision requiring VA venue may not be enforceable under CA law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisee may be able to litigate in CA instead of VA

overrides.override_summary: CA Corp Code §31512 voids waiver of rights under Franchise Investment Law; Bus & Prof Code §20010 voids waiver under Franchise Relations Act
overrides.why_it_matters: General releases required on renewal/transfer cannot waive CA franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: General release shall not relieve franchisor from liability imposed by Hawaii franchise laws
overrides.why_it_matters: Release language does not bar Hawaii franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Termination and non-renewal rights affected by IL law 815 ILCS 705/19 and 705/20
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchise law may provide additional termination/non-renewal protections

overrides.override_summary: IL Franchise Disclosure Act governs; FA provisions on governing law, jurisdiction, venue, jury waiver do not waive IL FDA rights
overrides.why_it_matters: IL law supersedes FA provisions inconsistent with IL FDA

overrides.override_summary: MD law may supersede FA in areas of termination and renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchise law provides additional protections on termination/renewal

overrides.override_summary: You may sue in MD for claims arising under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law. 3-year statute of limitations.
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees can bring franchise law claims in MD courts regardless of FA venue clause

overrides.override_summary: Release shall not relieve franchisor from liability under MD franchise law. No waiver of franchise law claims via questionnaire or acknowledgment.
overrides.why_it_matters: Strongest anti-waiver language of any state — covers all signed documents

overrides.override_summary: Termination before expiration only for good cause (failure to comply with lawful provisions + 30-day cure)
overrides.why_it_matters: MI law requires good cause for termination with cure opportunity — overrides some non-curable defaults

overrides.override_summary: Refusal to renew requires fair market value compensation for inventory, supplies, equipment, fixtures, furnishings (with exceptions)
overrides.why_it_matters: If term <5 years and franchisee can't continue similar business, franchisor must compensate on non-renewal

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration or litigation outside MI is void; franchisee can agree to out-of-state arbitration at time of arbitration
overrides.why_it_matters: VA venue requirement void for MI franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require release waiving MI Franchise Investment Law rights (but can settle post-FA claims)
overrides.why_it_matters: General release on renewal/transfer cannot cover MI franchise law rights

overrides.override_summary: 90-day notice of termination with 60-day cure; 180-day notice for non-renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly longer notice/cure periods than FA provides (FA: 10-30 days)

overrides.override_summary: MN Rule 2860.4400J prohibits requiring consent to liquidated damages
overrides.why_it_matters: LD provisions may be unenforceable against MN franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside MN or waiver of jury trial
overrides.why_it_matters: VA venue and any jury waiver provisions void for MN franchisees

overrides.override_summary: General release shall not relieve franchisor from MN Franchise Investment Law liability
overrides.why_it_matters: Release language cannot waive MN franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: NY Franchises Law requires NY law govern any cause of action under NY Franchises Law. NY GBL Article 33 §§680-695 may supersede inconsistent FA provisions.
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchise law supersedes where inconsistent, despite FA already designating NY law

overrides.override_summary: General release on renewal/transfer may not be enforceable under NY law
overrides.why_it_matters: Required general releases face enforceability challenges in NY

overrides.override_summary: All liquidated damages provisions deleted in their entirety
overrides.why_it_matters: LD provisions completely eliminated for ND franchisees — strongest protection of any state

overrides.override_summary: ND law supersedes NY law where in conflict. FA governed by ND law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Complete override of NY governing law for ND franchisees

overrides.override_summary: All venue designations outside ND deleted. Jury trial waiver deleted.
overrides.why_it_matters: VA venue and any jury waiver void; litigation must be in ND if ND franchisee brings action

overrides.override_summary: No release shall relieve franchisor from ND Franchise Disclosure Act liability
overrides.why_it_matters: Release language cannot waive ND franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Venue restriction to outside RI and choice of law other than RI void as to claims under RI Franchise Investment Act
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisees can bring RI FIA claims in RI courts under RI law

overrides.override_summary: Release on transfer or renewal does not apply to RI Franchise Investment Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchise law claims preserved despite general release

overrides.override_summary: VA Retail Franchising Act §13.1-564 — unlawful to cancel franchise without reasonable cause. FA termination grounds that don't constitute 'reasonable cause' may not be enforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: Some non-curable defaults may be challenged under VA reasonable cause standard

overrides.override_summary: WA RCW 19.100.180 may supersede FA on termination and renewal. Court decisions may also supersede.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA law provides additional termination/renewal protections

overrides.override_summary: Release excludes WA FIP Act rights unless negotiated settlement with independent counsel after FA in effect
overrides.why_it_matters: Standard form releases cannot waive WA franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in WA or mutually agreed. Litigation for WA FIP Act claims can be brought in WA.
overrides.why_it_matters: VA venue requirement overridden for WA franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void for employees earning <$100K/year and independent contractors earning <$250K/year (per RCW 49.62). No-poach provisions also void.
overrides.why_it_matters: Post-term noncompete (though already none in this FA) and any employee no-poach provisions unenforceable in WA
